title: UK elderly daily health check-in with fall-risk early detection

requirements.text: Implement an onboarding flow for older adults (65+) that captures a minimal user profile for fall-risk context: age (must be 65+), sex (optional), height/weight (optional), mobility aids used (e.g., cane/walker), history of falls in last 12 months (yes/no + count), key conditions (e.g., dizziness, arthritis, Parkinson’s—checkboxes), and medications that may affect balance (simple yes/no). Store this profile per authenticated Internet Identity user.

quotes: Create an app that is a daily check in for the elderly 65years and above, to check their health status and check if they are likely to have a fall.

acceptanceCriteria: Users can sign in with Internet Identity and complete onboarding.
Age < 65 is blocked with a clear English message explaining the app is for adults 65+.
Profile data is saved to the Motoko backend and is available on subsequent sessions.

requirements.text: Create a daily check-in form (one submission per calendar day per user, using UK local date) that collects health and fall-related signals such as: dizziness/lightheadedness, unsteadiness, new pain, fatigue/weakness, vision issues, medication changes today, alcohol intake, sleep quality, and whether the user had a near-fall or fall since last check-in. Persist each check-in entry to the backend with timestamp and UK date key.

quotes: daily check in ... check their health status and check if they are likely to have a fall

acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user can submit a check-in and see a confirmation screen.
The app prevents a second submission for the same UK date (with an option to view the submitted answers).
Check-ins are stored and retrievable from the backend for the signed-in user.

requirements.text: Implement a fall-risk assessment computed from the latest check-in (and optionally recent history like last 7 days) that outputs: a numeric risk score (0–100), a risk level (Low/Medium/High), and a short set of plain-English contributing factors (e.g., “Reported dizziness today”, “Near-fall reported”). The scoring must be deterministic, transparent (rules-based), and configurable in backend constants.

quotes: early detection tool for falls in the elderly

acceptanceCriteria: After submitting a check-in, the user sees risk score + level + contributing factors.
The backend exposes an API that returns the computed risk result for a given check-in.
Risk results for the same inputs are stable across reloads (no randomness).

requirements.text: Show tailored, non-alarming guidance based on the risk level, written for UK users in English (e.g., “Consider contacting your GP or NHS 111 for advice” for higher risk) and include a clear disclaimer that the app is not a medical device and does not provide a diagnosis or emergency response. Avoid claiming to predict falls with certainty.

quotes: The app should be an early detection tool for falls in the elderly in UK.

acceptanceCriteria: Low/Medium/High risk screens each show guidance appropriate to the level.
UI contains a persistent disclaimer (e.g., in results and settings/about).
Copy references UK-appropriate services (GP/NHS 111) without initiating calls/messages.

requirements.text: Add a history view that lets users review past check-ins and risk levels over time (e.g., last 30 entries) with accessible, high-contrast charts or list summaries and the ability to open a specific day’s details.

quotes: daily check in ... check their health status

acceptanceCriteria: User can view a list of previous check-ins with date and risk level.
Selecting an item shows the saved answers and computed risk details for that day.
Data is loaded via React Query with proper loading and error states.

requirements.text: Design the UI specifically for older adults: large default typography, high contrast, clear touch targets, simple language, and minimal steps. Include an accessibility settings panel with at least: text size (normal/large/largest) and contrast mode toggle, persisted locally in the browser.

quotes: daily check in for the elderly 65years and above

acceptanceCriteria: Primary screens meet a “large type” layout without clipping or overlapping.
Users can change text size and contrast mode and see the change immediately.
Settings persist across reloads (local storage is acceptable).

requirements.text: Create a coherent visual theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, components) suitable for a UK health check-in product and apply it consistently across onboarding, check-in, results, and history. Do not use a blue & purple palette.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a consistent theme applied across all pages.
Theme avoids blue & purple as the primary palette.
Components are visually consistent (buttons, cards, headings, alerts).

requirements.text: Backend data model and API: implement Motoko types and stable storage for user profile and check-ins, with query/update methods to (1) get/set profile, (2) create daily check-in, (3) list check-ins, (4) get computed risk for a check-in. Ensure data is isolated per authenticated principal.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can only read/write their own profile and check-ins (principal-based separation).
Backend methods cover the full frontend flow without requiring any external database.
Canister upgrade safety is considered using stable variables (and migration only if needed later).

requirements.text: Add basic input validation and friendly error handling: required fields in daily check-in must be validated client-side; backend must validate day uniqueness and reject invalid submissions; UI must present errors in plain English without exposing raw exceptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid or incomplete forms cannot be submitted and show inline messages.
Backend rejects duplicate same-day submissions and frontend shows an actionable message.
Network/backend failures show a retry option and do not blank the UI.

requirements.text: Add static informational pages: “About”, “How it works (rules-based risk)”, and “Privacy” explaining what data is stored in the canister, that no third-party services are used, and that the app is UK-focused. All text must be in English.

quotes: The app should be an early detection tool for falls in the elderly in UK.

acceptanceCriteria: Pages are accessible from navigation.
Copy explains data handling and limitations clearly in English.
No references to unsupported integrations (SMS, push notifications, external analytics).

requirements.text: Add generated static images to support branding and usability, and render them in the UI (e.g., on the landing/onboarding screens). Store them as static assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and do not serve images through the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist in frontend/public/assets/generated and are referenced in the React UI.
Images load correctly in production build without backend involvement.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in the main actor file).
Do not edit files under frontend immutablePaths (compose UI components instead).
No external databases or third-party auth; authentication must use Internet Identity.
Use English for all user-facing text.
UK-focused wording (e.g., GP/NHS 111) without implying emergency dispatch capabilities.
nonGoals: Not an emergency response system (no automatic calling, SMS, push notifications, or real-time monitoring).
Not a medical diagnosis or certified medical device; do not claim guaranteed fall prediction.
No caregiver/admin dashboard unless explicitly requested later.
No third-party analytics, social integrations, or external data sources.

required: Warm, reassuring app logo mark with simple symbol (steady-walk motif), flat design (uk-fallcheck-logo.dim_512x512.png)
Minimal hero illustration of an older adult doing a daily check-in on a tablet, clean flat style, high contrast (checkin-hero.dim_1600x900.png)
Set of 3 simple monochrome icons (dizzy, unsteady, sleep) in consistent stroke style (risk-icons-set.dim_1024x1024.png)

goalsToAdd: Build a UK-focused daily check-in app for adults 65+ to track health status and provide early detection of fall risk.
preferencesToAdd: Use English for all user-facing text.
